# 00 — Build Order

## Read this first if you're the Engineer

This document is the entry point. Read it, then read the specs in numerical order. Before writing any code, scan all eleven specs (00-10) end-to-end so the architecture makes sense as a whole.

**Critical: spec 08 lists every API detail in spec 04 that needs verification against current docs.** The Designer Claude wrote that spec from memory.

**Critical: there is no Anthropic API integration in Phase 1.** Coaching is generated by Sean using Claude Desktop with filesystem MCP — see spec 09. The dashboard's job ends at transcription.

**Critical: spec 10 (legal compliance) requires a recording disclosure to be the first thing on every call.** This is non-optional and changes the cockpit UI and the disposition enum. Read spec 10 alongside specs 03 and 04.

## The mission

Get Sean to "first call placed through phonebooth" by Sunday night. Monday morning he picks up the dashboard cold and starts dialing. Coaching happens in Claude Desktop afterwards.

## The specs

| # | Doc | What's in it |
|---|---|---|
| 00 | build-order.md | This file. Build sequence. |
| 01 | architecture.md | System overview, scope, data flow |
| 02 | data-model.md | SQLite schema for `leads`, `calls`, `events` |
| 03 | routes-controllers.md | Every HTTP route, every form, every controller method |
| 04 | twilio-integration.md | Browser softphone with code examples (verify against spec 08) |
| 05 | whisper-claude-integration.md | Whisper dual-channel transcription pipeline |
| 06 | targeting-brief.md | Reference only — what Phase 1 is about |
| 07 | logging-and-events.md | Application logs + events table |
| 08 | verification-checklist.md | API details to verify before building |
| 09 | claude-desktop-coaching.md | How Sean configures Claude Desktop for coaching |
| 10 | legal-compliance.md | Illinois recording consent — disclosure script + auto-delete behavior |

The skill in `docs/skills/01-jeb-blount.md` is loaded by Claude Desktop as project knowledge.

## Recommended build sequence

### Step 1: Scaffold (1 hour)

```bash
laravel new phonebooth --git
cd phonebooth
composer require twilio/sdk
npm install @twilio/voice-sdk
```

Create directories: `scripts/`, `storage/app/recordings/`, `storage/app/coaching/transcripts/`, `storage/app/coaching/feedback/`. Confirm `php artisan serve` works.

### Step 2: Data layer + logging foundation (1.5 hours)

Migrations and Eloquent models per specs 02 and 07:
- `create_leads_table` (spec 02)
- `create_calls_table` — note: includes `twilio_recording_sid` for spec 10's deletion API; does NOT include `coaching_feedback` or `coaching_framework`
- `create_events_table` (spec 07)

Configure the three logging channels per spec 07. Create `app/Services/EventLogger.php`.

Run `php artisan migrate`. Seed test leads.

Validate: tables exist, EventLogger writes a test row.

### Step 3: Leads UI (2-3 hours)

LeadController with index/show/update + import. Views. CSV import. No Twilio yet.

Validate: import a CSV, click into a lead, edit the brief, save.

### Step 4: Twilio infrastructure (2-4 hours)

**Before writing code: work through the Twilio sections of spec 08.**

Set up Twilio account and ngrok per spec 04. Configure `.env`. Implement TwilioTokenController. Build the cockpit page (calls.create) — **including the recording disclosure section at the top per spec 10.**

Disposition options on post-call form must include `declined_recording` (spec 10).

Add EventLogger calls per spec 07:
- `call_initiated` in `CallController::store()`
- `twilio_call_connected` in `TwilioTokenController::voice()`

Validate: load cockpit, see disclosure script. Click Call, talk to your own cell, hang up. Tinker check on events.

**Critical validation:** confirm CallSid in recording webhook matches `twilio_call_sid` column. If not, update matching logic.

### Step 5: Call data flow + spec 10 compliance (2 hours)

Implement CallController store/update. Build the post-call form. Implement the recording webhook with signature verification.

Add `twilio_recording_received` event in webhook handler — make sure to capture `RecordingSid` into the calls.twilio_recording_sid column.

**Implement the auto-delete-on-decline behavior per spec 10:**
- When disposition is set to `declined_recording` in CallController::update:
  - Delete local recording file
  - Issue Twilio DELETE API call (verify endpoint per spec 08)
  - Null out recording_url and recording_local_path
  - Record `consent_declined` and `recording_deleted` events

Validate: place a call, mark it `declined_recording`, verify the local file is deleted and the recording is gone from Twilio's console.

### Step 6: Whisper pipeline with dual-channel attribution (3-4 hours)

**Before writing code: work through the faster-whisper section of spec 08 AND the dual-channel splitting section.**

Install faster-whisper:
```bash
pip install faster-whisper
python3 -c "from faster_whisper import WhisperModel; WhisperModel('small', device='cpu', compute_type='int8')"
```

Verify ffmpeg channel splitting works (test command in spec 05's "First-run notes").

Create `scripts/transcribe.py` per spec 05 (note: outputs JSON now, not formatted text). Create:
- `RecordingDownloader.php`
- `ChannelSplitter.php` (NEW — splits stereo into two mono files)
- `Transcriber.php` (orchestrates: split → transcribe each → merge with speaker labels)

The Transcriber writes the merged transcript both to the DB AND to `storage/app/coaching/transcripts/{call_id}.md` for Claude Desktop.

Implement audio proxy route per spec 03.

Add EventLogger calls:
- `recording_downloaded` in RecordingDownloader on success
- `transcript_generated` in Transcriber on success (capture timing and segment counts)
- `error` events in catch blocks

Validate: place a call, click audio player on call detail (works via proxy). Click Process Call, see attributed transcript appear within ~2-3 minutes for a 5-min call. Verify the transcript shows `SEAN:` and `LEAD:` labels. Check the markdown file at `storage/app/coaching/transcripts/{call_id}.md`.

Make sure Process Call refuses to process calls with `disposition === 'declined_recording'`.

### Step 7: Filesystem MCP setup for Claude Desktop (30 min - 1 hour)

**Configuration on Sean's machine, not Engineer code.**

Engineer's job: confirm directory structure, document paths Sean needs.

Sean's job (per spec 09):
1. Configure filesystem MCP in `claude_desktop_config.json`
2. Create "Phonebooth Coaching" Project
3. Add Jeb Blount skill content as project knowledge
4. Add the workflow note as system prompt
5. Test by asking Claude to coach a sample transcript

Validate: Sean places a test call, processes it, opens Claude Desktop, asks "coach call 1," confirms feedback file appears at `storage/app/coaching/feedback/1.md`. Refresh dashboard call detail page, see coaching rendered.

### Step 8: Polish for Monday (1-2 hours)

- All error paths show useful messages AND record `error` events
- Test CSV import with weird rows
- Confirm ngrok URL is stable
- Place 3-4 test calls end-to-end including one declined-recording test
- Verify the disclosure script is visually prominent (it's the legal gate)

### Step 9: Pre-Monday (Sunday evening + earlier in week)

- Sean talks to an Illinois attorney about cold-calling compliance (per spec 10)
- Sean populates ten-lead CSV (spec 06 brief specifies sourcing)
- Practices the disclosure script out loud
- Imports leads
- Writes brief for the first lead
- Goes to bed

Note: previous spec said 50-lead CSV. That was scope creep. Ten leads/day per Sean's revised plan. The first morning of calling needs ten leads ready, not fifty.

## Total estimated time

15-20 hours of focused work. Slight increase from the previous estimate due to:
- Dual-channel splitting (spec 05): +30-60 min
- Auto-delete on declined_recording (spec 10): +30 min
- Disclosure section UI (spec 10): +15 min

## Critical paths

These will eat time if they go wrong:

1. **Twilio account verification + number purchase.** Saturday evening.
2. **ngrok stability.** Free tier rotates URLs.
3. **faster-whisper model download.** Pre-pull Saturday.
4. **Claude Desktop filesystem MCP setup.** Sean does Saturday or Sunday.
5. **Attorney consultation** for spec 10 compliance. Don't place the first call without this.
6. **ffmpeg dual-channel test.** Run the test command in spec 05 before processing real audio.

## Things fine to skip if time runs short

- Pretty styling
- "Add Lead" manual form
- Webhook signature verification on local dev
- Status webhook
- Comprehensive error handling beyond obvious cases

## Things NOT fine to skip

- Spec 08 verification of API details before steps 4 and 6
- Pain points field as required (spec 03)
- Recording webhook (entire downstream depends on it)
- ngrok tunnel running
- The coaching skill at `docs/skills/01-jeb-blount.md`
- Events table + EventLogger (spec 07)
- The eight required EventLogger call sites (spec 07)
- Audio proxy route
- Writing transcript markdown files for Claude Desktop
- **Disclosure section on cockpit page (spec 10)** — legal requirement, not a feature
- **Auto-delete on declined_recording (spec 10)** — legal protection
- **Dual-channel transcription with speaker attribution (spec 05)** — coaching quality depends on it

## When to ask for help

If any single step takes more than 2x its estimate, stop and surface it.

If spec 08's verification reveals something fundamentally broken architecturally, pause before working around it.

## A note on legal compliance

Spec 10 covers Illinois recording consent. It is operational guidance, not legal advice. Sean should consult a licensed Illinois attorney before placing the first call. The cost of a 30-minute consultation is far less than the cost of a felony eavesdropping charge (criminal penalty + civil damages).

The Engineer should implement spec 10 as written. Whether the implementation is legally sufficient is a question for Sean and his attorney, not the Engineer.

## A note on the Claude integration

There is no Claude API in this build. Spec 09 documents how Sean uses his Claude Desktop subscription via filesystem MCP for coaching. The dashboard's job ends at writing the transcript markdown file.

This was an architectural correction caught before build. The original spec had Anthropic API integration; that was removed in favor of MCP-based coaching for cost savings, simplicity, and a richer interactive coaching experience.

## A note on the events table

When something breaks Monday morning, the first place to look is the events table, not the Laravel logs. Tinker query for the call's events shows the timeline; Laravel logs show why a specific step failed.

See spec 07's "Sample debugging session."

## What success looks like Monday at 9 AM

Sean opens his laptop. He navigates to `localhost:8000/leads`. He sees ten Chicago retailers and trades. He clicks the first one. The brief is on the page. He puts on his headset.

The cockpit page loads. **At the top, in large text, is the disclosure script.** Sean reads it once silently to himself.

He clicks Call. The phone rings. Someone picks up.

Sean reads the disclosure: "Hi, this is Sean Roth calling. Quick note before we start — I record my calls and have an AI transcribe them so I can review my conversations and improve. Is that okay with you?"

The lead says "sure, go ahead." Sean continues with his pitch.

Some calls will end at the disclosure. That's data, not failure.

He hangs up. He fills in disposition, pain points, notes. He clicks Save. He clicks Process Call. Whisper transcribes both channels (~2-3 minutes). The transcript shows on the call detail page with `SEAN:` and `LEAD:` labels. The coaching section says "Not yet coached."

He continues for ten calls.

Mid-morning or afternoon, he opens Claude Desktop. He says "coach my unprocessed calls." Claude reads the attributed transcripts, generates coaching against the Jeb Blount framework, writes feedback files. Sean reads the feedback. He asks Claude follow-up questions on specific calls.

Tomorrow's "what worked, what didn't" question has answers. The pain_points field across calls feeds product discovery.

That's the system. Build for that.
